_doc: Project config — the one home for a project's choices, so the core depends on NO specific agent. The Orchestrator resolves a seat through `roles` before spawning and reads `mode` for decision authority. A swapped-in agent is bound by its role CONTRACT (PROTOCOL.md `## Role contracts`), not by being ours — the ship-gate checks the verdict's form, not its author. This file is the single home for what used to be a separate decision-authority marker, the project-kind marker, and an implicit role wiring. Absent file or unrecognised value ⇒ the safe defaults below (mode: interactive).
_mode: autonomous | interactive — value-home for PROTOCOL.md invariant 7. Absent or unrecognised ⇒ interactive (the safe default; never an error).
_platform: claude | opencode — which adapter realises the contract on this project. Usually auto-detected; pinned here when needed.
_kind: software | documentation — seeds the quality-layer defaults and the review route (a doc-kind project validates rather than code-reviews).
_roles: Each seat binds an `agent` and an optional `model`. `agent` = the id the active platform knows (a Claude subagent_type / an OpenCode agent id); swap it for ANY agent that honours the seat's contract — that is how you plug in a different reviewer (stricter, or an external engine) with zero core edit. `model` is ENVIRONMENT-DEPENDENT and the config states only the WISH; the platform adapter realises it: 'session' = same model as the orchestrator (cross-model off); 'auto' = a DIFFERENT model for independent blind spots (Claude switches to the other review-grade model, NEVER Haiku; OpenCode uses the adapter's configured cross-review model — which can be a wholly different provider/class); or a per-platform pin { "claude": "opus"|"sonnet", "opencode": "provider/model" } for an explicit class. Omitted ⇒ 'session', EXCEPT the reviewer defaults to 'auto' (a maker-model can't see its own blind spots). ZERO-CONFIG: 'auto' just works — on Claude it picks the opposite of the two models; on OpenCode, which has no guaranteed second model, it falls back to 'session' (one model for everything) until you opt in. The model ALLOWLIST + auto-policy live in the platform adapter (tool-map.json `models`); a pin must be allow-listed.
